Plan intense outdoor sessions before 8 AM or after 5:30 PM because Chennai heat and humidity can raise fatigue quickly. No injury history was entered, so the plan starts with normal beginner-safe checks. Target calories: 2570. Protein: 101 grams. Water: 2.9 litres. Monday. Aerobic Base. Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility Main: Aerobic Base work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing Banana with water Idli with sambar Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure Tender coconut water after heavy sweating Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness. mentor: Balance stamina with ball work so fitness transfers directly into match decisions.
Use small-sided games for scanning, passing timing, pressing, and recovery runs.
Review one tactical theme each week: positioning, transition, pressing, or finishing.

Add single-leg strength for acceleration, deceleration, and knee control.

Two days out: use light touches, passing rhythm, and set-piece rehearsal.
Game day: eat early, hydrate steadily, and warm up with progressive sprints.
equipment: Studs or turf shoes, shin guards, ball, cones, bibs
tactics: Scan before receiving the ball
Create passing angles after every pass
Press as a unit and recover shape quickly
injuryPrevention: Keep ankle and hip mobility consistent
nutrition: Use carb-rich meals 3-4 hours before matches
Hydrate before warm-up, not only after sweating
Recover with protein and fluids within 60 minutes

videoMentors.title: Football tournament preparation
videoMentors.topic: Match readiness, warm-up, and pressure practice

recovery: Sleep 7-9 hours when possible; recovery is part of training.
Keep at least one low-intensity day after hard intervals or match play.
Use 5-10 minutes of mobility for hips, ankles, thoracic spine, and shoulders.
If sharp pain appears, stop the session and reassess before continuing.
safetyChecks: Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness.
Do not train through sharp pain or swelling.
Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually.
Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food.

progressPlan.target: Complete 4 planned sessions each week
Update once weekly, same time of day
Log a simple 1-5 score after each training day
Log a simple 1-5 score after each training day
Log a simple 1-5 score after each training day

dailyChallenges.title: Five-minute warm-up streak
dailyChallenges.description: Before every Football session, finish mobility, breathing, and easy movement first.

dailyChallenges.title: Skill touch challenge
dailyChallenges.description: Spend 10 focused minutes on one technical drill before conditioning.

dailyChallenges.title: Recovery check-in
dailyChallenges.description: Rate sleep, soreness, hydration, and energy before increasing intensity.

dailyChallenges.title: Coach note
dailyChallenges.description: Write one thing that improved and one thing to practice next session.

roadmap.mentorSummary: pragadeesh, உங்கள் 2 நாள் திட்டம் Gym training பயிற்சியுடன் Lose fat இலக்குக்காக உருவாக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது. சென்னையின் வெப்பம் மற்றும் ஈரப்பதம் காரணமாக கடினமான வெளிப்புற பயிற்சியை காலை 8 மணிக்கு முன் அல்லது மாலை 5:30க்கு பிறகு செய்யுங்கள். உங்கள் காயம் குறிப்பு: "knee pain". எனவே வலியில்லாத இயக்கமும் மெதுவான முன்னேற்றமும் முதன்மை.

session: வார்ம் அப்: 8 நிமிடம் வேக நடை அல்லது மெதுவான ஜாக், பிறகு டைனமிக் மொபிலிட்டி
முக்கியம்: கூட்டு பலம் பயிற்சி 25-35 நிமிடம், easy to moderate தீவிரத்தில்
பலப் பயிற்சி: Home/bodyweight பயன்படுத்தி 3 சுற்றுகள் ஸ்குவாட், லஞ்ச், புஷ்-அப், ரோ, பிளாங்க்
கண்டிஷனிங்: 30 விநாடி வேக முயற்சி + 90 விநாடி மெதுவான மீட்பு, 6 சுற்றுகள்
கூல் டவுன்: 5 நிமிடம் நடை மற்றும் மெதுவான மூச்சுப் பயிற்சி
வார்ம் அப்: 8 நிமிடம் வேக நடை அல்லது மெதுவான ஜாக், பிறகு டைனமிக் மொபிலிட்டி
முக்கியம்: தசை வளர்ச்சி பயிற்சி 25-35 நிமிடம், easy to moderate தீவிரத்தில்
பலப் பயிற்சி: Home/bodyweight பயன்படுத்தி 3 சுற்றுகள் ஸ்குவாட், லஞ்ச், புஷ்-அப், ரோ, பிளாங்க்
திறன்: முழு ஓய்வுடன் 15 நிமிடம் கட்டுப்படுத்தப்பட்ட டெக்னிக் பயிற்சி
கூல் டவுன்: 5 நிமிடம் நடை மற்றும் மெதுவான மூச்சுப் பயிற்சி
20-30 நிமிடம் எளிய நடை
இடுப்பு, கால்வலி பகுதி, மார்பு, தோள் ஆகியவற்றுக்கு லைட் ஸ்ட்ரெச்சிங்
எடை, தூக்கம், தசை வலி, பயிற்சி குறிப்புகள் ஆகியவற்றை மதிப்பாய்வு செய்யுங்கள்

preWorkout: பயிற்சிக்கு 60 நிமிடம் மேல் இருந்தால் சிறிய தோசை
postWorkout: சாதம் அல்லது சப்பாத்தியுடன் முட்டை, சிக்கன், மீன், தயிர், பருப்பு, பனீர் அல்லது சன்னா
வெப்பத்தால் பசி குறைந்தால் தயிர் சாதம் அல்லது மோர்
அதிக வியர்வைக்கு பிறகு இளநீர்
dailyMeals: ஒவ்வொரு உணவிலும் புரதம், சாதம் அல்லது சப்பாத்தி, காய்கறி, சிறிது நல்ல கொழுப்பு சேர்க்கவும்
வறுத்த ஸ்நாக்ஸ் மற்றும் இனிப்பு பானங்களை குறைக்கவும்
கிராஷ் டயட் வேண்டாம்; அளவுகளை மெதுவாக சரிசெய்து வாராந்திர முன்னேற்றத்தை பாருங்கள்

languageSupport.note: வழிகாட்டி உரைக்கு Tamil, குரல் உரையாடல்களுக்கு Tamil பயன்படுத்தப்படும்.

roadmap.voiceScript: pragadeesh, உங்கள் 2 நாள் திட்டம் Gym training பயிற்சியுடன் Lose fat இலக்குக்காக உருவாக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது. சென்னையின் வெப்பம் மற்றும் ஈரப்பதம் காரணமாக கடினமான வெளிப்புற பயிற்சியை காலை 8 மணிக்கு முன் அல்லது மாலை 5:30க்கு பிறகு செய்யுங்கள். உங்கள் காயம் குறிப்பு: "knee pain". எனவே வலியில்லாத இயக்கமும் மெதுவான முன்னேற்றமும் முதன்மை. இலக்கு கலோரி: 2110. புரதம்: 134 கிராம். தண்ணீர்: 3.4 லிட்டர். திங்கள். கூட்டு பலம். வார்ம் அப்: 8 நிமிடம் வேக நடை அல்லது மெதுவான ஜாக், பிறகு டைனமிக் மொபிலிட்டி முக்கியம்: கூட்டு பலம் பயிற்சி 25-35 நிமிடம், easy to moderate தீவிரத்தில் பலப் பயிற்சி: Home/bodyweight பயன்படுத்தி 3 சுற்றுகள் ஸ்குவாட், லஞ்ச், புஷ்-அப், ரோ, பிளாங்க் கண்டிஷனிங்: 30 விநாடி வேக முயற்சி + 90 விநாடி மெதுவான மீட்பு, 6 சுற்றுகள் கூல் டவுன்: 5 நிமிடம் நடை மற்றும் மெதுவான மூச்சுப் பயிற்சி தண்ணீருடன் வாழைப்பழம் சாம்பாருடன் இட்லி பயிற்சிக்கு 60 நிமிடம் மேல் இருந்தால் சிறிய தோசை சாதம் அல்லது சப்பாத்தியுடன் முட்டை, சிக்கன், மீன், தயிர், பருப்பு, பனீர் அல்லது சன்னா வெப்பத்தால் பசி குறைந்தால் தயிர் சாதம் அல்லது மோர் அதிக வியர்வைக்கு பிறகு இளநீர் மார்பு வலி, மயக்கம், கடுமையான மூச்சுத்திணறல் அல்லது தலைசுற்றல் இருந்தால் உடனே மருத்துவ உதவி பெறுங்கள். கூர்மையான வலி அல்லது வீக்கம் இருந்தால் பயிற்சி செய்ய வேண்டாம். ஓட்ட தூரம் அல்லது எடைப் பயிற்சி சுமையை மெதுவாக மட்டுமே அதிகரிக்கவும். சப்பிளிமெண்ட்ஸ் கட்டாயமில்லை; சாதாரண உணவை மாற்றக்கூடாது.

mentor: Anchor the week around compound lifts, accessory balance, and recovery.
Progress only one variable at a time: load, reps, sets, or tempo.
Use a training log for technique notes, load, reps, and readiness.
fitness: Prioritize squat, hinge, push, pull, carry, and core patterns.
Use conditioning finishers only after strength quality is complete.
Keep mobility for hips, shoulders, ankles, and thoracic spine.
tournament: For physique or strength events: peak technique first, then reduce fatigue.
Seven days out: avoid max attempts unless planned by a coach.
Event day: warm up gradually and keep attempts or sets within the plan.
equipment: Dumbbells, barbell, plates, bench, cable or bands
Lifting belt only when technique is stable
tactics: Prioritize clean reps before heavier load
Plan push, pull, squat, hinge, core, and carry patterns
Use deload weeks before fatigue builds too high
injuryPrevention: Warm up movement patterns, not only muscles
Avoid max attempts when sleep or readiness is poor
nutrition: Keep protein consistent each day
Use carbs around hard lifting
Hydrate well before long gym sessions

videoMentors.title: Gym training tournament preparation
videoMentors.topic: Match readiness, warm-up, and pressure practice

recovery: முடிந்தால் 7-9 மணி நேரம் தூங்குங்கள்; மீட்பும் பயிற்சியின் ஒரு பகுதி.
கடின இடைவெளி பயிற்சி அல்லது போட்டிக்குப் பிறகு குறைந்த தீவிர நாள் வைத்துக்கொள்ளுங்கள்.
இடுப்பு, கணுக்கால், மேல் முதுகு, தோள் ஆகியவற்றுக்கு 5-10 நிமிடம் மொபிலிட்டி செய்யுங்கள்.
பழைய காய வலி திரும்பினால் தீவிரத்தை குறைத்து பிஸியோதெரபிஸ்டை அணுகுங்கள்.
safetyChecks: மார்பு வலி, மயக்கம், கடுமையான மூச்சுத்திணறல் அல்லது தலைசுற்றல் இருந்தால் உடனே மருத்துவ உதவி பெறுங்கள்.
கூர்மையான வலி அல்லது வீக்கம் இருந்தால் பயிற்சி செய்ய வேண்டாம்.
ஓட்ட தூரம் அல்லது எடைப் பயிற்சி சுமையை மெதுவாக மட்டுமே அதிகரிக்கவும்.
சப்பிளிமெண்ட்ஸ் கட்டாயமில்லை; சாதாரண உணவை மாற்றக்கூடாது.

progressPlan.target: Complete 2 planned sessions each week
Update once weekly, same time of day
Log a simple 1-5 score after each training day
Log a simple 1-5 score after each training day
Log a simple 1-5 score after each training day

dailyChallenges.title: Five-minute warm-up streak
dailyChallenges.description: Before every Gym training session, finish mobility, breathing, and easy movement first.

dailyChallenges.title: Skill touch challenge
dailyChallenges.description: Spend 10 focused minutes on one technical drill before conditioning.

dailyChallenges.title: Recovery check-in
dailyChallenges.description: Rate sleep, soreness, hydration, and energy before increasing intensity.

dailyChallenges.title: Coach note
dailyChallenges.description: Write one thing that improved and one thing to practice next session.

கூல் டவுன்: 5 நிமிடம் நடை மற்றும் மெதுவான மூச்சுப் பயிற்சி
20-30 நிமிடம் எளிய நடை
இடுப்பு, கால்வலி பகுதி, மார்பு, தோள் ஆகியவற்றுக்கு லைட் ஸ்ட்ரெச்சிங்
எடை, தூக்கம், தசை வலி, பயிற்சி குறிப்புகள் ஆகியவற்றை மதிப்பாய்வு செய்யுங்கள்

preWorkout: பயிற்சிக்கு 60 நிமிடம் மேல் இருந்தால் சிறிய தோசை
postWorkout: சாதம் அல்லது சப்பாத்தியுடன் முட்டை, சிக்கன், மீன், தயிர், பருப்பு, பனீர் அல்லது சன்னா
வெப்பத்தால் பசி குறைந்தால் தயிர் சாதம் அல்லது மோர்
அதிக வியர்வைக்கு பிறகு இளநீர்
dailyMeals: ஒவ்வொரு உணவிலும் புரதம், சாதம் அல்லது சப்பாத்தி, காய்கறி, சிறிது நல்ல கொழுப்பு சேர்க்கவும்
வறுத்த ஸ்நாக்ஸ் மற்றும் இனிப்பு பானங்களை குறைக்கவும்
கிராஷ் டயட் வேண்டாம்; அளவுகளை மெதுவாக சரிசெய்து வாராந்திர முன்னேற்றத்தை பாருங்கள்

languageSupport.note: வழிகாட்டி உரைக்கு Tamil, குரல் உரையாடல்களுக்கு Tamil பயன்படுத்தப்படும்.

roadmap.voiceScript: umass, உங்கள் 2 நாள் திட்டம் Gym training பயிற்சியுடன் Build muscle இலக்குக்காக உருவாக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது. சென்னையின் வெப்பம் மற்றும் ஈரப்பதம் காரணமாக கடினமான வெளிப்புற பயிற்சியை காலை 8 மணிக்கு முன் அல்லது மாலை 5:30க்கு பிறகு செய்யுங்கள். காயம் வரலாறு இல்லை, எனவே சாதாரண பாதுகாப்பான தொடக்க திட்டம் கொடுக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது. இலக்கு கலோரி: 2430. புரதம்: 108 கிராம். தண்ணீர்: 2.5 லிட்டர். திங்கள். கூட்டு பலம். வார்ம் அப்: 8 நிமிடம் வேக நடை அல்லது மெதுவான ஜாக், பிறகு டைனமிக் மொபிலிட்டி முக்கியம்: கூட்டு பலம் பயிற்சி 25-35 நிமிடம், easy to moderate தீவிரத்தில் பலப் பயிற்சி: Home/bodyweight பயன்படுத்தி 3 சுற்றுகள் ஸ்குவாட், லஞ்ச், புஷ்-அப், ரோ, பிளாங்க் கண்டிஷனிங்: 30 விநாடி வேக முயற்சி + 90 விநாடி மெதுவான மீட்பு, 6 சுற்றுகள் கூல் டவுன்: 5 நிமிடம் நடை மற்றும் மெதுவான மூச்சுப் பயிற்சி தண்ணீருடன் வாழைப்பழம் சாம்பாருடன் இட்லி பயிற்சிக்கு 60 நிமிடம் மேல் இருந்தால் சிறிய தோசை சாதம் அல்லது சப்பாத்தியுடன் முட்டை, சிக்கன், மீன், தயிர், பருப்பு, பனீர் அல்லது சன்னா வெப்பத்தால் பசி குறைந்தால் தயிர் சாதம் அல்லது மோர் அதிக வியர்வைக்கு பிறகு இளநீர் மார்பு வலி, மயக்கம், கடுமையான மூச்சுத்திணறல் அல்லது தலைசுற்றல் இருந்தால் உடனே மருத்துவ உதவி பெறுங்கள். கூர்மையான வலி அல்லது வீக்கம் இருந்தால் பயிற்சி செய்ய வேண்டாம். ஓட்ட தூரம் அல்லது எடைப் பயிற்சி சுமையை மெதுவாக மட்டுமே அதிகரிக்கவும். சப்பிளிமெண்ட்ஸ் கட்டாயமில்லை; சாதாரண உணவை மாற்றக்கூடாது.

mentor: Anchor the week around compound lifts, accessory balance, and recovery.
Progress only one variable at a time: load, reps, sets, or tempo.
Use a training log for technique notes, load, reps, and readiness.
fitness: Prioritize squat, hinge, push, pull, carry, and core patterns.
Use conditioning finishers only after strength quality is complete.
Keep mobility for hips, shoulders, ankles, and thoracic spine.
tournament: For physique or strength events: peak technique first, then reduce fatigue.
Seven days out: avoid max attempts unless planned by a coach.
Event day: warm up gradually and keep attempts or sets within the plan.
equipment: Dumbbells, barbell, plates, bench, cable or bands
Lifting belt only when technique is stable
tactics: Prioritize clean reps before heavier load
Plan push, pull, squat, hinge, core, and carry patterns
Use deload weeks before fatigue builds too high
injuryPrevention: Warm up movement patterns, not only muscles
Avoid max attempts when sleep or readiness is poor
nutrition: Keep protein consistent each day
Use carbs around hard lifting
Hydrate well before long gym sessions

videoMentors.title: Gym training tournament preparation
videoMentors.topic: Match readiness, warm-up, and pressure practice

recovery: முடிந்தால் 7-9 மணி நேரம் தூங்குங்கள்; மீட்பும் பயிற்சியின் ஒரு பகுதி.
கடின இடைவெளி பயிற்சி அல்லது போட்டிக்குப் பிறகு குறைந்த தீவிர நாள் வைத்துக்கொள்ளுங்கள்.
இடுப்பு, கணுக்கால், மேல் முதுகு, தோள் ஆகியவற்றுக்கு 5-10 நிமிடம் மொபிலிட்டி செய்யுங்கள்.
கூர்மையான வலி இருந்தால் பயிற்சியை நிறுத்தி மீண்டும் மதிப்பாய்வு செய்யுங்கள்.
safetyChecks: மார்பு வலி, மயக்கம், கடுமையான மூச்சுத்திணறல் அல்லது தலைசுற்றல் இருந்தால் உடனே மருத்துவ உதவி பெறுங்கள்.
கூர்மையான வலி அல்லது வீக்கம் இருந்தால் பயிற்சி செய்ய வேண்டாம்.
ஓட்ட தூரம் அல்லது எடைப் பயிற்சி சுமையை மெதுவாக மட்டுமே அதிகரிக்கவும்.
சப்பிளிமெண்ட்ஸ் கட்டாயமில்லை; சாதாரண உணவை மாற்றக்கூடாது.

roadmap.mentorSummary: Aarav, this 5-day plan is built for sport performance through football training. Plan intense outdoor sessions before 8 AM or after 5:30 PM because Chennai heat and humidity can raise fatigue quickly. No injury history was entered, so the plan starts with normal beginner-safe checks.

session: Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility
Main: Aerobic Base work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity
Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight
Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery
Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing
Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility
Main: Sprint Intervals work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity
Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight
Skill: 15 minutes of controlled technique practice with full recovery between efforts
Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing
Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility
Main: Agility work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity
Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight
Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery
Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing
Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility
Main: Single-leg Strength work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity
Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight
Skill: 15 minutes of controlled technique practice with full recovery between efforts
Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing
Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility
Main: Aerobic Base work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity
Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight
Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery
Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing
20-30 minutes easy walk
Light stretching for hips, calves, chest, and shoulders
Review weight, sleep, soreness, and training notes

preWorkout: Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away
postWorkout: Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa
Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure
Tender coconut water after heavy sweating
dailyMeals: Build each meal around protein, rice or chapati, vegetables, and a small amount of healthy fat

roadmap.voiceScript: Aarav, this 4-day plan is built for running performance through running training near Velachery. Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. No injury history was entered, so the plan starts with normal beginner-safe checks. Target calories: 2570. Protein: 115 grams. Water: 3.2 litres. Monday. Easy Mileage. Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility Main: Easy Mileage work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing Chennai note: Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. Banana with water Idli with sambar Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure Tender coconut water after heavy sweating This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis. Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness. Do not train through sharp pain or swelling. Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually. Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food. Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets. For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

mentor: Keep most running easy so the hard sessions are high quality.
Use one speed or tempo session weekly based on the goal.
Track distance, pace, perceived effort, sleep, soreness, and heat stress.
fitness: Build aerobic base with easy runs and gradual weekly volume increases.
Add strength for calves, glutes, hamstrings, and core two times per week.
Use strides after easy runs to improve form without heavy fatigue.
tournament: Two weeks out: taper volume while keeping short fast efforts.
Three days out: avoid new workouts, shoes, food, or routes.
Race day: start controlled, hydrate by weather, and finish with a planned effort increase.
equipment: Running shoes matched to comfort and surface
Watch or phone timer for pacing
tactics: Start easier than target pace
Use effort-based pacing in heat
Practice race-day fueling before the event
injuryPrevention: Rotate easy days after hard sessions
nutrition: Use familiar carbs before long runs
Replace fluids and sodium in humid weather
Eat protein and carbs after long or fast sessions

roadmap.voiceScript: Aarav, this 5-day plan is built for sport performance through football training near Velachery. Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. No injury history was entered, so the plan starts with normal beginner-safe checks. Target calories: 2910. Protein: 115 grams. Water: 3.2 litres. Monday. Aerobic Base. Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility Main: Aerobic Base work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing Chennai note: Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. Banana with water Idli with sambar Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure Tender coconut water after heavy sweating This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis. Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness. Do not train through sharp pain or swelling. Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually. Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food. Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets. For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

mentor: Balance stamina with ball work so fitness transfers directly into match decisions.
Use small-sided games for scanning, passing timing, pressing, and recovery runs.
Review one tactical theme each week: positioning, transition, pressing, or finishing.
fitness: Alternate aerobic base runs with sprint intervals and change-of-direction work.
Add single-leg strength for acceleration, deceleration, and knee control.
Keep mobility for hips, ankles, hamstrings, and calves after every session.
tournament: Ten days out: make sessions more match-like and reduce extra conditioning.
Two days out: use light touches, passing rhythm, and set-piece rehearsal.
Game day: eat early, hydrate steadily, and warm up with progressive sprints.
equipment: Studs or turf shoes, shin guards, ball, cones, bibs
tactics: Scan before receiving the ball
Create passing angles after every pass
Press as a unit and recover shape quickly
injuryPrevention: Keep ankle and hip mobility consistent
nutrition: Use carb-rich meals 3-4 hours before matches
Hydrate before warm-up, not only after sweating
Recover with protein and fluids within 60 minutes

recovery: Sleep 7-9 hours when possible; recovery is part of training.
Keep at least one low-intensity day after hard intervals or match play.
Use 5-10 minutes of mobility for hips, ankles, thoracic spine, and shoulders.
If sharp pain appears, stop the session and reassess before continuing.
safetyChecks: This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis.
Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness.
Do not train through sharp pain or swelling.
Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually.
Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food.
Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets.
For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

roadmap.mentorSummary: Aarav, this 5-day plan is built for sport performance through football training near Velachery. Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. No injury history was entered, so the plan starts with normal beginner-safe checks.

session: Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility
Main: Aerobic Base work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity
Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight
Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery
Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing
Chennai note: Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress.
Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility
Main: Sprint Intervals work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity
Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight
Skill: 15 minutes of controlled technique practice with full recovery between efforts
Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing
Chennai note: Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress.

Main: Agility work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity
Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight
Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery
Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing

Main: Single-leg Strength work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity

preWorkout: Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away
postWorkout: Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa
Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure
Tender coconut water after heavy sweating
dailyMeals: Build each meal around protein, rice or chapati, vegetables, and a small amount of healthy fat
Keep fried snacks and sugary drinks occasional, especially during fat-loss phases
Do not use crash diets; adjust portions gradually and track weekly progress

languageSupport.note: Use English for mentor text and English for voice conversations when the browser supports this language.

roadmap.voiceScript: Aarav, this 5-day plan is built for sport performance through football training near Velachery. Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. No injury history was entered, so the plan starts with normal beginner-safe checks. Target calories: 2910. Protein: 115 grams. Water: 3.2 litres. Monday. Aerobic Base. Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility Main: Aerobic Base work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing Chennai note: Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. Banana with water Idli with sambar Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure Tender coconut water after heavy sweating This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis. Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness. Do not train through sharp pain or swelling. Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually. Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food. Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets. For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

mentor: Balance stamina with ball work so fitness transfers directly into match decisions.
Use small-sided games for scanning, passing timing, pressing, and recovery runs.
Review one tactical theme each week: positioning, transition, pressing, or finishing.
fitness: Alternate aerobic base runs with sprint intervals and change-of-direction work.
Add single-leg strength for acceleration, deceleration, and knee control.
Keep mobility for hips, ankles, hamstrings, and calves after every session.
tournament: Ten days out: make sessions more match-like and reduce extra conditioning.
Two days out: use light touches, passing rhythm, and set-piece rehearsal.
Game day: eat early, hydrate steadily, and warm up with progressive sprints.
equipment: Studs or turf shoes, shin guards, ball, cones, bibs
tactics: Scan before receiving the ball
Create passing angles after every pass
Press as a unit and recover shape quickly
injuryPrevention: Keep ankle and hip mobility consistent
nutrition: Use carb-rich meals 3-4 hours before matches
Hydrate before warm-up, not only after sweating
Recover with protein and fluids within 60 minutes

Do not train through sharp pain or swelling.

postWorkout: Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa

languageSupport.note: Use English for mentor text and English for voice conversations when the browser supports this language.

roadmap.voiceScript: Aarav, this 3-day plan is built for stay healthy through football training near Velachery. Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. No injury history was entered, so the plan starts with normal beginner-safe checks. Target calories: 2310. Protein: 101 grams. Water: 3.2 litres. Monday. Aerobic Base. Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility Main: Aerobic Base work for 25-35 minutes at easy to moderate intensity Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing Chennai note: Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. Banana with water Idli with sambar Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure Tender coconut water after heavy sweating This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis. Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness. Do not train through sharp pain or swelling. Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually. Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food. Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets. For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

mentor: Balance stamina with ball work so fitness transfers directly into match decisions.
Use small-sided games for scanning, passing timing, pressing, and recovery runs.
Review one tactical theme each week: positioning, transition, pressing, or finishing.
fitness: Alternate aerobic base runs with sprint intervals and change-of-direction work.
Add single-leg strength for acceleration, deceleration, and knee control.
Keep mobility for hips, ankles, hamstrings, and calves after every session.
tournament: Ten days out: make sessions more match-like and reduce extra conditioning.
Two days out: use light touches, passing rhythm, and set-piece rehearsal.
Game day: eat early, hydrate steadily, and warm up with progressive sprints.
equipment: Studs or turf shoes, shin guards, ball, cones, bibs
tactics: Scan before receiving the ball
Create passing angles after every pass
Press as a unit and recover shape quickly
injuryPrevention: Keep ankle and hip mobility consistent
nutrition: Use carb-rich meals 3-4 hours before matches
Hydrate before warm-up, not only after sweating
Recover with protein and fluids within 60 minutes

videoMentors.title: Football tournament preparation
videoMentors.topic: Match readiness, warm-up, and pressure practice

recovery: Sleep 7-9 hours when possible; recovery is part of training.
Keep at least one low-intensity day after hard intervals or match play.
Use 5-10 minutes of mobility for hips, ankles, thoracic spine, and shoulders.
If sharp pain appears, stop the session and reassess before continuing.
safetyChecks: This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis.
Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness.
Do not train through sharp pain or swelling.
Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually.
Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food.
Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets.
For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

preWorkout: Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away
postWorkout: Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa
Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure
Tender coconut water after heavy sweating
dailyMeals: Build each meal around protein, rice or chapati, vegetables, and a small amount of healthy fat
Keep fried snacks and sugary drinks occasional, especially during fat-loss phases
Do not use crash diets; adjust portions gradually and track weekly progress

languageSupport.note: Use English for mentor text and English for voice conversations when the browser supports this language.

roadmap.voiceScript: Aarav, this 4-day plan is built for improve stamina through football training near Velachery. Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. No injury history was entered, so the plan starts with normal beginner-safe checks. Target calories: 2570. Protein: 101 grams. Water: 3.2 litres. Monday. Aerobic Base. Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility Main: Aerobic Base work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing Chennai note: Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. Banana with water Idli with sambar Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure Tender coconut water after heavy sweating This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis. Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness. Do not train through sharp pain or swelling. Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually. Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food. Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets. For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

mentor: Balance stamina with ball work so fitness transfers directly into match decisions.
Use small-sided games for scanning, passing timing, pressing, and recovery runs.
Review one tactical theme each week: positioning, transition, pressing, or finishing.
fitness: Alternate aerobic base runs with sprint intervals and change-of-direction work.
Add single-leg strength for acceleration, deceleration, and knee control.
Keep mobility for hips, ankles, hamstrings, and calves after every session.
tournament: Ten days out: make sessions more match-like and reduce extra conditioning.
Two days out: use light touches, passing rhythm, and set-piece rehearsal.
Game day: eat early, hydrate steadily, and warm up with progressive sprints.
equipment: Studs or turf shoes, shin guards, ball, cones, bibs
tactics: Scan before receiving the ball
Create passing angles after every pass
Press as a unit and recover shape quickly
injuryPrevention: Keep ankle and hip mobility consistent
nutrition: Use carb-rich meals 3-4 hours before matches
Hydrate before warm-up, not only after sweating
Recover with protein and fluids within 60 minutes

videoMentors.title: Football tournament preparation
videoMentors.topic: Match readiness, warm-up, and pressure practice

recovery: Sleep 7-9 hours when possible; recovery is part of training.
Keep at least one low-intensity day after hard intervals or match play.
Use 5-10 minutes of mobility for hips, ankles, thoracic spine, and shoulders.
If sharp pain appears, stop the session and reassess before continuing.
safetyChecks: This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis.
Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness.
Do not train through sharp pain or swelling.
Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually.
Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food.
Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets.
For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

roadmap.voiceScript: Aarav, this 5-day plan is built for lose fat through football training near Velachery. Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. No injury history was entered, so the plan starts with normal beginner-safe checks. Target calories: 2220. Protein: 115 grams. Water: 3.2 litres. Monday. Aerobic Base. Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility Main: Aerobic Base work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing Chennai note: Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. Banana with water Idli with sambar Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure Tender coconut water after heavy sweating This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis. Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness. Do not train through sharp pain or swelling. Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually. Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food. Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets. For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

mentor: Balance stamina with ball work so fitness transfers directly into match decisions.
Use small-sided games for scanning, passing timing, pressing, and recovery runs.
Review one tactical theme each week: positioning, transition, pressing, or finishing.
fitness: Alternate aerobic base runs with sprint intervals and change-of-direction work.
Add single-leg strength for acceleration, deceleration, and knee control.
Keep mobility for hips, ankles, hamstrings, and calves after every session.
tournament: Ten days out: make sessions more match-like and reduce extra conditioning.
Two days out: use light touches, passing rhythm, and set-piece rehearsal.
Game day: eat early, hydrate steadily, and warm up with progressive sprints.
equipment: Studs or turf shoes, shin guards, ball, cones, bibs
tactics: Scan before receiving the ball
Create passing angles after every pass
Press as a unit and recover shape quickly
injuryPrevention: Keep ankle and hip mobility consistent
nutrition: Use carb-rich meals 3-4 hours before matches
Hydrate before warm-up, not only after sweating
Recover with protein and fluids within 60 minutes

videoMentors.title: Football tournament preparation
videoMentors.topic: Match readiness, warm-up, and pressure practice

recovery: Sleep 7-9 hours when possible; recovery is part of training.
Keep at least one low-intensity day after hard intervals or match play.
Use 5-10 minutes of mobility for hips, ankles, thoracic spine, and shoulders.
If sharp pain appears, stop the session and reassess before continuing.
safetyChecks: This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis.
Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness.
Do not train through sharp pain or swelling.
Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually.
Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food.
Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets.
For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

postWorkout: Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa

roadmap.voiceScript: Aarav, this 4-day plan is built for sport performance through cricket training near Velachery. Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. No injury history was entered, so the plan starts with normal beginner-safe checks. Target calories: 2310. Protein: 115 grams. Water: 3.2 litres. Monday. Sprint Mechanics. Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility Main: Sprint Mechanics work for 25-35 minutes at easy to moderate intensity Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing Chennai note: Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. Banana with water Idli with sambar Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure Tender coconut water after heavy sweating This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis. Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness. Do not train through sharp pain or swelling. Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually. Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food. Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets. For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

mentor: Build every week around batting or bowling skill blocks, fielding reactions, and shoulder care.
Keep one focused net session for match scenario practice instead of only volume hitting.
Track decision quality: shot choice, bowling line/length, field awareness, and calmness under pressure.
fitness: Sprint 10-30 metres with full recovery for quick singles and fielding bursts.
Train rotational core strength with medicine-ball throws, band chops, and anti-rotation holds.
Use shoulder prehab before every throwing or bowling session.
tournament: Two weeks out: reduce heavy strength volume and increase match simulation.
Three days out: keep training short, sharp, and skill-focused.
Game day: hydrate early, warm up shoulders thoroughly, and review role-specific cues.
equipment: Bat, ball, stumps, gloves, pads, helmet, abdominal guard
Supportive shoes or spikes based on surface
tactics: Rotate strike instead of forcing every ball
Bowl to a field plan, not only to the batter
Protect shoulders by managing throwing and bowling volume
injuryPrevention: Warm up rotator cuff and thoracic spine
Use landing mechanics and calf strength for fast bowling
nutrition: Use light carbs before nets
Add electrolytes during long Chennai sessions
Recover with protein plus rice, chapati, or fruit
mistakes: Ignoring fielding speed and catching volume

recovery: Sleep 7-9 hours when possible; recovery is part of training.
Keep at least one low-intensity day after hard intervals or match play.
Use 5-10 minutes of mobility for hips, ankles, thoracic spine, and shoulders.
If sharp pain appears, stop the session and reassess before continuing.
safetyChecks: This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis.
Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness.
Do not train through sharp pain or swelling.
Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually.
Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food.
Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets.
For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness. Do not train through sharp pain or swelling. For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

mentor: Build every week around batting or bowling skill blocks, fielding reactions, and shoulder care.
Keep one focused net session for match scenario practice instead of only volume hitting.
Track decision quality: shot choice, bowling line/length, field awareness, and calmness under pressure.
fitness: Sprint 10-30 metres with full recovery for quick singles and fielding bursts.
Train rotational core strength with medicine-ball throws, band chops, and anti-rotation holds.
Use shoulder prehab before every throwing or bowling session.
tournament: Two weeks out: reduce heavy strength volume and increase match simulation.
Three days out: keep training short, sharp, and skill-focused.
Game day: hydrate early, warm up shoulders thoroughly, and review role-specific cues.
equipment: Bat, ball, stumps, gloves, pads, helmet, abdominal guard
Supportive shoes or spikes based on surface
tactics: Rotate strike instead of forcing every ball
Bowl to a field plan, not only to the batter
Protect shoulders by managing throwing and bowling volume
injuryPrevention: Warm up rotator cuff and thoracic spine
Use landing mechanics and calf strength for fast bowling
nutrition: Use light carbs before nets
Add electrolytes during long Chennai sessions
Recover with protein plus rice, chapati, or fruit
mistakes: Ignoring fielding speed and catching volume

preWorkout: Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away
postWorkout: Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa
Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure
Tender coconut water after heavy sweating
dailyMeals: Build each meal around protein, rice or chapati, vegetables, and a small amount of healthy fat
Keep fried snacks and sugary drinks occasional, especially during fat-loss phases
Do not use crash diets; adjust portions gradually and track weekly progress

languageSupport.note: Use English for mentor text and English for voice conversations when the browser supports this language.

roadmap.voiceScript: Aarav, this 5-day plan is built for sport performance through cricket training near Velachery. Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. No injury history was entered, so the plan starts with normal beginner-safe checks. Target calories: 2910. Protein: 115 grams. Water: 3.2 litres. Monday. Sprint Mechanics. Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility Main: Sprint Mechanics work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing Chennai note: Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. Banana with water Idli with sambar Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure Tender coconut water after heavy sweating This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis. Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness. Do not train through sharp pain or swelling. Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually. Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food. Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets. For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

mentor: Build every week around batting or bowling skill blocks, fielding reactions, and shoulder care.
Keep one focused net session for match scenario practice instead of only volume hitting.
Track decision quality: shot choice, bowling line/length, field awareness, and calmness under pressure.
fitness: Sprint 10-30 metres with full recovery for quick singles and fielding bursts.
Train rotational core strength with medicine-ball throws, band chops, and anti-rotation holds.
Use shoulder prehab before every throwing or bowling session.
tournament: Two weeks out: reduce heavy strength volume and increase match simulation.
Three days out: keep training short, sharp, and skill-focused.
Game day: hydrate early, warm up shoulders thoroughly, and review role-specific cues.
equipment: Bat, ball, stumps, gloves, pads, helmet, abdominal guard
Supportive shoes or spikes based on surface
tactics: Rotate strike instead of forcing every ball
Bowl to a field plan, not only to the batter
Protect shoulders by managing throwing and bowling volume
injuryPrevention: Warm up rotator cuff and thoracic spine
Use landing mechanics and calf strength for fast bowling
nutrition: Use light carbs before nets
Add electrolytes during long Chennai sessions
Recover with protein plus rice, chapati, or fruit
mistakes: Ignoring fielding speed and catching volume

videoMentors.topic: Throwing accuracy ladder + Slip catching reactions

videoMentors.title: Cricket tournament preparation
videoMentors.topic: Match readiness, warm-up, and pressure practice

recovery: Sleep 7-9 hours when possible; recovery is part of training.
Keep at least one low-intensity day after hard intervals or match play.
Use 5-10 minutes of mobility for hips, ankles, thoracic spine, and shoulders.
If sharp pain appears, stop the session and reassess before continuing.
safetyChecks: This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis.
Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness.
Do not train through sharp pain or swelling.
Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually.
Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food.
Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets.
For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

preWorkout: Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away
postWorkout: Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa
Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure
Tender coconut water after heavy sweating
dailyMeals: Build each meal around protein, rice or chapati, vegetables, and a small amount of healthy fat
Keep fried snacks and sugary drinks occasional, especially during fat-loss phases
Do not use crash diets; adjust portions gradually and track weekly progress

languageSupport.note: Use English for mentor text and English for voice conversations when the browser supports this language.

roadmap.voiceScript: umass, this 5-day plan is built for sport performance through cricket training near Velachery. Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. No injury history was entered, so the plan starts with normal beginner-safe checks. Target calories: 2780. Protein: 104 grams. Water: 2.9 litres. Monday. Sprint Mechanics. Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility Main: Sprint Mechanics work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing Chennai note: Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. Banana with water Idli with sambar Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure Tender coconut water after heavy sweating This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis. Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness. Do not train through sharp pain or swelling. Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually. Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food. Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets. For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

mentor: Build every week around batting or bowling skill blocks, fielding reactions, and shoulder care.
Keep one focused net session for match scenario practice instead of only volume hitting.
Track decision quality: shot choice, bowling line/length, field awareness, and calmness under pressure.
fitness: Sprint 10-30 metres with full recovery for quick singles and fielding bursts.
Train rotational core strength with medicine-ball throws, band chops, and anti-rotation holds.
Use shoulder prehab before every throwing or bowling session.
tournament: Two weeks out: reduce heavy strength volume and increase match simulation.
Three days out: keep training short, sharp, and skill-focused.
Game day: hydrate early, warm up shoulders thoroughly, and review role-specific cues.
equipment: Bat, ball, stumps, gloves, pads, helmet, abdominal guard
Supportive shoes or spikes based on surface
tactics: Rotate strike instead of forcing every ball
Bowl to a field plan, not only to the batter
Protect shoulders by managing throwing and bowling volume
injuryPrevention: Warm up rotator cuff and thoracic spine
Use landing mechanics and calf strength for fast bowling
nutrition: Use light carbs before nets
Add electrolytes during long Chennai sessions
Recover with protein plus rice, chapati, or fruit
mistakes: Ignoring fielding speed and catching volume

videoMentors.topic: Throwing accuracy ladder + Slip catching reactions

videoMentors.title: Cricket tournament preparation
videoMentors.topic: Match readiness, warm-up, and pressure practice

recovery: Sleep 7-9 hours when possible; recovery is part of training.
Keep at least one low-intensity day after hard intervals or match play.
Use 5-10 minutes of mobility for hips, ankles, thoracic spine, and shoulders.
If sharp pain appears, stop the session and reassess before continuing.
safetyChecks: This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis.
Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness.
Do not train through sharp pain or swelling.
Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually.
Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food.
Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets.
For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

preWorkout: Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away
postWorkout: Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa
Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure
Tender coconut water after heavy sweating
dailyMeals: Build each meal around protein, rice or chapati, vegetables, and a small amount of healthy fat
Keep fried snacks and sugary drinks occasional, especially during fat-loss phases
Do not use crash diets; adjust portions gradually and track weekly progress

languageSupport.note: Use English for mentor text and English for voice conversations when the browser supports this language.

roadmap.voiceScript: Aarav, this 4-day plan is built for improve strength through gym training training near Velachery. Keep hydration visible even indoors because Chennai humidity can still raise sweat loss. Your injury note says "Knee pain, Shoulder pain, Doctor advised caution", so pain-free movement and conservative progression come first. Target calories: 2820. Protein: 130 grams. Water: 3.2 litres. Monday. Compound Strength. Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility Main: Compound Strength work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight, Dumbbells, Resistance band, Bench, Foam roller Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing Chennai note: Keep hydration visible even indoors because Chennai humidity can still raise sweat loss. Banana with water Idli with sambar Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure Tender coconut water after heavy sweating This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis. Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness. Do not train through sharp pain or swelling. Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually. Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food. Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets. For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

mentor: Anchor the week around compound lifts, accessory balance, and recovery.
Progress only one variable at a time: load, reps, sets, or tempo.
Use a training log for technique notes, load, reps, and readiness.
fitness: Prioritize squat, hinge, push, pull, carry, and core patterns.
Use conditioning finishers only after strength quality is complete.
Keep mobility for hips, shoulders, ankles, and thoracic spine.
tournament: For physique or strength events: peak technique first, then reduce fatigue.
Seven days out: avoid max attempts unless planned by a coach.
Event day: warm up gradually and keep attempts or sets within the plan.
equipment: Dumbbells, barbell, plates, bench, cable or bands
Lifting belt only when technique is stable
tactics: Prioritize clean reps before heavier load
Plan push, pull, squat, hinge, core, and carry patterns
Use deload weeks before fatigue builds too high
injuryPrevention: Warm up movement patterns, not only muscles
Avoid max attempts when sleep or readiness is poor
nutrition: Keep protein consistent each day
Use carbs around hard lifting
Hydrate well before long gym sessions

videoMentors.title: Gym training tournament preparation
videoMentors.topic: Match readiness, warm-up, and pressure practice

recovery: Sleep 7-9 hours when possible; recovery is part of training.
Keep at least one low-intensity day after hard intervals or match play.
Use 5-10 minutes of mobility for hips, ankles, thoracic spine, and shoulders.
If old injury pain returns, reduce intensity and consult a physiotherapist.
safetyChecks: This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis.
Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness.
Do not train through sharp pain or swelling.
Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually.
Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food.
Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets.
For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

postWorkout: Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa

roadmap.voiceScript: Aarav, this 5-day plan is built for sport performance through football training near Velachery. Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. No injury history was entered, so the plan starts with normal beginner-safe checks. Target calories: 2910. Protein: 115 grams. Water: 3.2 litres. Monday. Aerobic Base. Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility Main: Aerobic Base work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing Chennai note: Early morning is the best Chennai window for harder outdoor sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. Banana with water Idli with sambar Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure Tender coconut water after heavy sweating This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis. Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness. Do not train through sharp pain or swelling. Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually. Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food. Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets. For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

mentor: Balance stamina with ball work so fitness transfers directly into match decisions.
Use small-sided games for scanning, passing timing, pressing, and recovery runs.
Review one tactical theme each week: positioning, transition, pressing, or finishing.
fitness: Alternate aerobic base runs with sprint intervals and change-of-direction work.
Add single-leg strength for acceleration, deceleration, and knee control.
Keep mobility for hips, ankles, hamstrings, and calves after every session.
tournament: Ten days out: make sessions more match-like and reduce extra conditioning.
Two days out: use light touches, passing rhythm, and set-piece rehearsal.
Game day: eat early, hydrate steadily, and warm up with progressive sprints.
equipment: Studs or turf shoes, shin guards, ball, cones, bibs
tactics: Scan before receiving the ball
Create passing angles after every pass
Press as a unit and recover shape quickly
injuryPrevention: Keep ankle and hip mobility consistent
nutrition: Use carb-rich meals 3-4 hours before matches
Hydrate before warm-up, not only after sweating
Recover with protein and fluids within 60 minutes

videoMentors.title: Football tournament preparation
videoMentors.topic: Match readiness, warm-up, and pressure practice

recovery: Sleep 7-9 hours when possible; recovery is part of training.
Keep at least one low-intensity day after hard intervals or match play.
Use 5-10 minutes of mobility for hips, ankles, thoracic spine, and shoulders.
If sharp pain appears, stop the session and reassess before continuing.
safetyChecks: This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis.

Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually.
Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food.
Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets.

roadmap.mentorSummary: Aarav, this 10-session weekly plan is built for improve endurance through running training near Mumbai. Your injury note says "No current injury", so pain-free movement and conservative progression come first.

preWorkout: Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away
postWorkout: Rice or chapati with eggs, dal, channa, sprouts, curd, soy chunks, peanuts, or seasonal home food
Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure
Tender coconut water after heavy sweating
dailyMeals: Build each meal around protein, rice or chapati, vegetables, and a small amount of healthy fat
Keep fried snacks and sugary drinks occasional, especially during fat-loss phases
Do not use crash diets; adjust portions gradually and track weekly progress

languageSupport.note: Use English for mentor text and English for voice conversations when the browser supports this language.

roadmap.voiceScript: Aarav, this 10-session weekly plan is built for improve endurance through running training near Mumbai. Midday outdoor training in Mumbai can be high risk in hot or humid weather, so shift intense work before 8 AM or after 5:30 PM and keep midday training indoors or very light. Your injury note says "No current injury", so pain-free movement and conservative progression come first. Target calories: 2570. Protein: 115 grams. Water: 3.6 litres. Monday. Easy Mileage. Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility Main: Easy Mileage work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Running shoes, Foam roller Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing India weather note: Midday outdoor training in Mumbai can be high risk in hot or humid weather, so shift intense work before 8 AM or after 5:30 PM and keep midday training indoors or very light. Banana with water Idli with sambar Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away Rice or chapati with eggs, dal, channa, sprouts, curd, soy chunks, peanuts, or seasonal home food Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure Tender coconut water after heavy sweating This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis. Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness. Do not train through sharp pain or swelling. Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually. Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food. Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets. For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

mentor: Keep most running easy so the hard sessions are high quality.
Use one speed or tempo session weekly based on the goal.
Track distance, pace, perceived effort, sleep, soreness, and heat stress.
fitness: Build aerobic base with easy runs and gradual weekly volume increases.
Add strength for calves, glutes, hamstrings, and core two times per week.
Use strides after easy runs to improve form without heavy fatigue.
tournament: Two weeks out: taper volume while keeping short fast efforts.
Three days out: avoid new workouts, shoes, food, or routes.
Race day: start controlled, hydrate by weather, and finish with a planned effort increase.
equipment: Running shoes matched to comfort and surface
Watch or phone timer for pacing
tactics: Start easier than target pace
Use effort-based pacing in heat
Practice race-day fueling before the event
injuryPrevention: Rotate easy days after hard sessions
nutrition: Use familiar carbs before long runs
Replace fluids and sodium in humid weather
Eat protein and carbs after long or fast sessions

videoMentors.title: Running tournament preparation
videoMentors.topic: Match readiness, warm-up, and pressure practice

recovery: Sleep 7-9 hours when possible; recovery is part of training.
Keep at least one low-intensity day after hard intervals or match play.
Use 5-10 minutes of mobility for hips, ankles, thoracic spine, and shoulders.
If old injury pain returns, reduce intensity and consult a physiotherapist.
safetyChecks: This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis.
Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness.
Do not train through sharp pain or swelling.
Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually.
Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food.
Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets.
For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

preWorkout: Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away
postWorkout: Rice or chapati with paneer, curd, dal, sprouts, channa, rajma, or tofu
Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure
Tender coconut water after heavy sweating
dailyMeals: Build each meal around protein, rice or chapati, vegetables, and a small amount of healthy fat
Keep fried snacks and sugary drinks occasional, especially during fat-loss phases
Do not use crash diets; adjust portions gradually and track weekly progress

languageSupport.note: Use English for mentor text and English for voice conversations when the browser supports this language.

roadmap.voiceScript: Aarav, this 14-session weekly plan is built for build muscle through gym training training near thanjavur. Early morning is usually the best outdoor window in India for harder sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. Your injury note says "No current injury", so pain-free movement and conservative progression come first. Target calories: 2820. Protein: 130 grams. Water: 3.2 litres. Monday. Compound Strength. Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility Main: Compound Strength work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight, Dumbbells, Kettlebell, Barbell, Gym machines Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing India weather note: Early morning is usually the best outdoor window in India for harder sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. Banana with water Idli with sambar Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away Rice or chapati with paneer, curd, dal, sprouts, channa, rajma, or tofu Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure Tender coconut water after heavy sweating This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis. Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness. Do not train through sharp pain or swelling. Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually. Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food. Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets. For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

mentor: Anchor the week around compound lifts, accessory balance, and recovery.
Progress only one variable at a time: load, reps, sets, or tempo.
Use a training log for technique notes, load, reps, and readiness.
fitness: Prioritize squat, hinge, push, pull, carry, and core patterns.
Use conditioning finishers only after strength quality is complete.
Keep mobility for hips, shoulders, ankles, and thoracic spine.
tournament: For physique or strength events: peak technique first, then reduce fatigue.
Seven days out: avoid max attempts unless planned by a coach.
Event day: warm up gradually and keep attempts or sets within the plan.
equipment: Dumbbells, barbell, plates, bench, cable or bands
Lifting belt only when technique is stable
tactics: Prioritize clean reps before heavier load
Plan push, pull, squat, hinge, core, and carry patterns
Use deload weeks before fatigue builds too high
injuryPrevention: Warm up movement patterns, not only muscles
Avoid max attempts when sleep or readiness is poor
nutrition: Keep protein consistent each day
Use carbs around hard lifting
Hydrate well before long gym sessions

videoMentors.title: Gym training tournament preparation
videoMentors.topic: Match readiness, warm-up, and pressure practice

recovery: Sleep 7-9 hours when possible; recovery is part of training.
Keep at least one low-intensity day after hard intervals or match play.
Use 5-10 minutes of mobility for hips, ankles, thoracic spine, and shoulders.
If old injury pain returns, reduce intensity and consult a physiotherapist.
safetyChecks: This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis.
Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness.
Do not train through sharp pain or swelling.
Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually.
Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food.
Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets.
For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

preWorkout: Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away
postWorkout: Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa
Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure
Tender coconut water after heavy sweating
dailyMeals: Build each meal around protein, rice or chapati, vegetables, and a small amount of healthy fat
Keep fried snacks and sugary drinks occasional, especially during fat-loss phases
Do not use crash diets; adjust portions gradually and track weekly progress

languageSupport.note: Use English for mentor text and English for voice conversations when the browser supports this language.

roadmap.voiceScript: Aarav, this 4-session weekly plan is built for healthy weight gain through football training near Bengaluru. Early morning is usually the best outdoor window in India for harder sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. No injury history was entered, so the plan starts with normal beginner-safe checks. Target calories: 2920. Protein: 130 grams. Water: 3.2 litres. Monday. Aerobic Base. Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility Main: Aerobic Base work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing India weather note: Early morning is usually the best outdoor window in India for harder sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. Banana with water Idli with sambar Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure Tender coconut water after heavy sweating This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis. Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness. Do not train through sharp pain or swelling. Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually. Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food. Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets. For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

mentor: Balance stamina with ball work so fitness transfers directly into match decisions.
Use small-sided games for scanning, passing timing, pressing, and recovery runs.
Review one tactical theme each week: positioning, transition, pressing, or finishing.
fitness: Alternate aerobic base runs with sprint intervals and change-of-direction work.
Add single-leg strength for acceleration, deceleration, and knee control.
Keep mobility for hips, ankles, hamstrings, and calves after every session.
tournament: Ten days out: make sessions more match-like and reduce extra conditioning.
Two days out: use light touches, passing rhythm, and set-piece rehearsal.
Game day: eat early, hydrate steadily, and warm up with progressive sprints.
equipment: Studs or turf shoes, shin guards, ball, cones, bibs
tactics: Scan before receiving the ball
Create passing angles after every pass
Press as a unit and recover shape quickly
injuryPrevention: Keep ankle and hip mobility consistent
nutrition: Use carb-rich meals 3-4 hours before matches
Hydrate before warm-up, not only after sweating
Recover with protein and fluids within 60 minutes

videoMentors.title: Football tournament preparation
videoMentors.topic: Match readiness, warm-up, and pressure practice

recovery: Sleep 7-9 hours when possible; recovery is part of training.
Keep at least one low-intensity day after hard intervals or match play.
Use 5-10 minutes of mobility for hips, ankles, thoracic spine, and shoulders.
If sharp pain appears, stop the session and reassess before continuing.
safetyChecks: This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis.
Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness.
Do not train through sharp pain or swelling.
Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually.
Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food.
Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets.
For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

baselineGuidance.label: Baseline
baselineGuidance.detail: Aim for 150-300 minutes of moderate cardio each week plus 4 strength sessions. This plan turns that baseline into simple weekly steps.
