preWorkout: Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away
postWorkout: Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa
Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure
Tender coconut water after heavy sweating
dailyMeals: Build each meal around protein, rice or chapati, vegetables, and a small amount of healthy fat
Keep fried snacks and sugary drinks occasional, especially during fat-loss phases
Do not use crash diets; adjust portions gradually and track weekly progress

languageSupport.note: Use English for mentor text and English for voice conversations when the browser supports this language.

roadmap.voiceScript: umashankar, this 14-session weekly plan is built for gain weight through general fitness training near thanjavur. Early morning is usually the best outdoor window in India for harder sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. Your injury note says "No current injury", so pain-free movement and conservative progression come first. Target calories: 2800. Protein: 110 grams. Water: 2.8 litres. Monday. Full-body Strength. Warm-up: 8 minutes brisk walk or light jog, then dynamic mobility Main: Full-body Strength work for 25-35 minutes at moderate intensity Strength: 3 rounds of squats, lunges, push-ups, rows, and plank using Home/bodyweight, Gym machines Conditioning: 6 rounds of 30-second fast effort plus 90-second easy recovery Cooldown: 5 minutes walking and slow breathing India weather note: Early morning is usually the best outdoor window in India for harder sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. Banana with water Idli with sambar Small dosa with chutney if training is more than 60 minutes away Rice or chapati with eggs, chicken, fish, curd, dal, paneer, or channa Curd rice or buttermilk when appetite is low after heat exposure Tender coconut water after heavy sweating This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis. Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness. Do not train through sharp pain or swelling. Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually. Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food. Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets. For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

mentor: Blend strength, cardio, mobility, and habit consistency.
Choose repeatable sessions first, then slowly increase difficulty.
Measure progress with energy, movement quality, sleep, and consistency.
fitness: Use full-body strength two to three times per week.
Add zone 2 cardio for heart health and stamina.
Keep daily mobility short and consistent.
tournament: For any event or challenge: practice the exact format before increasing intensity.
One week out: reduce soreness and keep movement familiar.
Event day: warm up gently, pace early, and finish with control.
tactics: Build consistency before intensity
Mix strength, cardio, and mobility
Choose simple habits that fit the week
nutrition: Build meals around protein and vegetables
Keep hydration visible through the day
Avoid crash diets for quick changes
mistakes: Trying to change food and training too aggressively

videoMentors.title: General fitness practice drills
videoMentors.topic: Full-body circuit + Zone 2 walk or cycle

videoMentors.title: General fitness tournament preparation
videoMentors.topic: Match readiness, warm-up, and pressure practice

recovery: Sleep 7-9 hours when possible; recovery is part of training.
Keep at least one low-intensity day after hard intervals or match play.
Use 5-10 minutes of mobility for hips, ankles, thoracic spine, and shoulders.
If old injury pain returns, reduce intensity and consult a physiotherapist.
safetyChecks: This is fitness guidance, not a medical diagnosis.
Stop exercise and seek medical help for chest pain, faintness, severe breathlessness, or dizziness.
Do not train through sharp pain or swelling.
Increase weekly running volume or lifting load gradually.
Supplements are optional and should not replace normal food.
Do not use steroids, banned substances, or crash diets.
For pregnancy, chronic illness, heart symptoms, or active injuries, consult a doctor, physiotherapist, or certified coach.

baselineGuidance.label: India
baselineGuidance.detail: Early morning is usually the best outdoor window in India for harder sessions; still carry water and watch for heat stress. Hydration is adjusted for thanjavur, training time, heat, and humidity.

baselineGuidance.label: Safety
baselineGuidance.detail: Logged notes include "No current injury". Keep the next session easy if symptoms are active, and get qualified help for red flags.

baselineGuidance.label: Baseline
baselineGuidance.detail: Aim for 150-300 minutes of moderate cardio each week plus 4 strength sessions. This plan turns that baseline into simple weekly steps.

progressPlan.target: Complete 14 planned sessions each week
Update once weekly, same time of day
Log a simple 1-5 score after each training day
Log a simple 1-5 score after each training day
Log a simple 1-5 score after each training day

dailyChallenges.title: Five-minute warm-up streak
dailyChallenges.description: Before every General fitness session, finish mobility, breathing, and easy movement first.

dailyChallenges.title: Skill touch challenge
dailyChallenges.description: Spend 10 focused minutes on one technical drill before conditioning.

dailyChallenges.title: Recovery check-in
dailyChallenges.description: Rate sleep, soreness, hydration, and energy before increasing intensity.

dailyChallenges.title: Coach note
dailyChallenges.description: Write one thing that improved and one thing to practice next session.
